citations.narrative:
The facility maintains a large aviary structure that contains numerous primary enclosures of various sizes. Some small
enclosures house only breeding pairs while other large enclosures house more than ten flighted birds. The licensee is
currently housing a colony of seventeen domestic cats in the aviary that are not considered regulated animals. The cats
are temporarily housed in several large primary enclosures directly adjacent to enclosures housing regulated birds
separated by transparent wire screen dividers. The inspector observed an adult cat jumping onto the screened divider
separating the cat enclosure from a neighboring bird enclosure. This type of activity has the potential to cause injury or
undue stress to the regulated birds in the adjacent enclosure. Having mixed species in the same housing environment
may lead to interactions that are hazardous to their health and well-being.
Birds may not be housed with other animals, including members of their own species, unless they are compatible, do not
prevent access to food, water, or shelter by individual animals, and are known not to be hazardous to the health and well-
being of each other. Compatibility must be determined in accordance with generally accepted professional practices. The
licensee elected to relocate the cats to an enclosure not associated with the regulated collection.
To be corrected by March 17, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
3.160(b) 
   
Compatibility and separation 
The facility maintains a large aviary structure that contains numerous primary enclosures of various sizes. Some small 
enclosures house only breeding pairs while other large enclosures house more than ten flighted birds. The licensee is 
currently housing a colony of seventeen domestic cats in the aviary that are not considered regulated animals. The cats 
are temporarily housed in several large primary enclosures directly adjacent to enclosures housing regulated birds 
separated by transparent wire screen dividers. The inspector observed an adult cat jumping onto the screened divider 
separating the cat enclosure from a neighboring bird enclosure. This type of activity has the potential to cause injury or 
undue stress to the regulated birds in the adjacent enclosure. Having mixed species in the same housing environment 
may lead to interactions that are hazardous to their health and well-being. 
 
Birds may not be housed with other animals, including members of their own species, unless they are compatible, do not 
prevent access to food, water, or shelter by individual animals, and are known not to be hazardous to the health and well-
being of each other. Compatibility must be determined in accordance with generally accepted professional practices. The 
licensee elected to relocate the cats to an enclosure not associated with the regulated collection. 
 
To be corrected by March 17, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee. 
 
 n
